methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 13 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Viken Detection is a Series C defense & security company that has raised $50M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 13 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage companies, comprised of 2 public comparables and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Saronic, and Shield AI. Public references include DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression approach, applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $50M raised proxy, implies a $85M valuation. The peer-median method references the median valuation across the 13-company comp set at $1.2B, with a range spanning $300M (low) to $9.2B (high, Saronic at Series D). This wider dispersion reflects both stage variance—Series B peers like Vannevar Labs and Dream Security at $1.5B versus Series D peers like Chaos Industries at $4.5B—and underlying business-model differentiation within the defense sector.

The two methods diverge substantially, yielding a triangulated range of $72M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $668M. The stage-progression method anchors the conservative bound, while peer comparables drive the upper bound. Key uncertainties include company-specific revenue traction, customer concentration risk, and whether Viken's product differentiation and sales trajectory more closely resemble earlier-stage or later-stage comp trajectories. Further due diligence on growth metrics and unit economics is required to narrow the range.
